matter.title: Business asset sale: Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. — Grace C. Roe to Maria Q. Fairbanks

jurisdiction.court_type: Private closing / Maine Secretary of State

matter.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks is acquiring the assets of Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. (a precision machine shop) from Grace C. Roe for $6897794 — about 3.5x EBITDA of $2477406 — via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase), with closing set for 2026-05-16.

fact_pattern.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks is acquiring the assets of Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. (a precision machine shop) from Grace C. Roe for $6897794 — about 3.5x EBITDA of $2477406 — via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase), with closing set for 2026-05-16.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe owns Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc., a precision machine shop operating as a member-managed LLC for 19 years with 19 employees, about $4169227 in revenue and $2477406 in adjusted EBITDA. Maria Q. Fairbanks has agreed to buy substantially all of its assets (machinery, WIP inventory, and the trade name) through an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase) for $6897794, roughly a 3.5x multiple, into a new entity, Downeast Dental Partners LLC.

The deal includes a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing against a $214992 target, a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price, a 2-year earnout of up to $400,000 tied to gross-profit retention, and no seller financing. Buyer's CPA is running a quality-of-earnings review by 2026-03-06, testing related-party rent normalized to market and normalizing the numbers. Buyer is financing with a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital of about $7573808; the price is allocated under an IRC § 1060 purchase-price allocation on Form 8594 across Class I-VII assets.

Only specified trade payables and customer deposits are assumed; the transaction contemplates a new 10-year lease with the seller as landlord and a 3-year non-compete plus a 1-year consulting agreement. Counsel Ingrid B. Kingsley of Hawthorne Legal Group is drafting the APA, escrow and non-compete agreements, bill of sale and assignments, and coordinating the lender and the Form 8594 allocation for a 2026-05-16 closing.
undisputed_facts: Maria Q. Fairbanks is buying the assets of Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. from Grace C. Roe for $6897794 via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase).
Adjusted EBITDA is about $2477406 on $4169227 of revenue.
The deal includes a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price and a 3-year non-compete plus a 1-year consulting agreement.
disputed_facts: The quality of earnings and which related-party rent normalized to market are legitimate normalizations.
The closing working-capital peg and the a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing.
The purchase-price allocation on Form 8594 (goodwill vs. equipment) and its tax effect on each side.

third_parties.name: Maine Secretary of State
third_parties.relevance: Registers the buyer's acquisition entity and any name filings.

third_parties.name: Desmond D. Poe
third_parties.relevance: Performs the financial due diligence and QoE analysis.

third_parties.name: Riverbend Analytics LLC
third_parties.relevance: Marketed the business and manages the deal process.

third_parties.name: Granite State Credit Union
third_parties.relevance: Underwrites the acquisition loan against cash flow and collateral.

third_parties.name: Maria S. Ashford
third_parties.relevance: Valued the business and allocated assets for Form 8594.

exchanges.question: What are you buying?
exchanges.answer: The assets of Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc., a precision machine shop, for $6897794 via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase).

exchanges.question: How did you price it?
exchanges.answer: About 3.5x on $2477406 adjusted EBITDA, after normalizing related-party rent normalized to market.

exchanges.question: Any holdback, earnout, or seller note?
exchanges.answer: a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price, a 2-year earnout of up to $400,000 tied to gross-profit retention, and no seller financing.

exchanges.question: How is working capital handled?
exchanges.answer: a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing against a $214992 target.

exchanges.question: How is it financed?
exchanges.answer: a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital of about $7573808, into Downeast Dental Partners LLC.

exchanges.question: What's your accountant checking?
exchanges.answer: A quality-of-earnings review by 2026-03-06 — revenue recognition, add-backs, and the balance sheet.

exchanges.question: What about competition and liabilities?
exchanges.answer: a 3-year non-compete plus a 1-year consulting agreement, and we're assuming only specified trade payables and customer deposits.

goals: Close the asset purchase at $6897794 into Downeast Dental Partners LLC on 2026-05-16.
Confirm quality of earnings and the working-capital peg before the 2026-03-06.
Lock financing (a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital) and a favorable Form 8594 allocation.
priorities: Verified earnings and a clean balance sheet at closing.
desired_outcomes: A funded asset acquisition with escrow, a working-capital true-up, and clear title to the assets.
non_starters: Assuming undisclosed liabilities or unfunded tax exposure.
Closing before the QoE and financing are firm.

constraints.budget: $6897794 funded by a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital of $7573808 plus equity and no seller financing.

issues.issue: Assignment of contracts, leases, and licenses
sub_questions: Do key contracts and a new 10-year lease with the seller as landlord require third-party consent?

issues.issue: Enforceability of the non-compete and non-solicit
issues.governing_law: 26 M.R.S. § 599-A (noncompete limits); reasonableness at common law
sub_questions: Are the a 3-year non-compete plus a 1-year consulting agreement scope, duration, and geography reasonable?

issues.issue: Working-capital adjustment and closing balance sheet
sub_questions: How are disputes over the closing statement resolved?
Is a Maine tax clearance / bulk-sale notice needed?

amounts.label: Acquisition loan
amounts.basis: a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital.

amounts.label: Purchase price (asset deal)
amounts.basis: About 3.5x adjusted EBITDA via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase).

amounts.label: Adjusted EBITDA
amounts.basis: Normalized for related-party rent normalized to market.

communications.subject: Signed LOI — Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc.
communications.summary: Broker circulates the executed LOI and opens the data room.
communications.body: Attached is the signed LOI for the assets of Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. at $6897794 (~3.5x on $2477406 adjusted EBITDA), via an asset purchase (not a stock/equity purchase) into Downeast Dental Partners LLC. Terms include a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price, a 2-year earnout of up to $400,000 tied to gross-profit retention, no seller financing, and a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing. The data room now holds three years of financials and tax returns, the customer list, and the asset schedule.

communications.subject: Financial due-diligence request — Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc.
communications.summary: Buyer's accountant sends the accounting/financing diligence questions.
communications.body: To complete our quality-of-earnings review by 2026-03-06, please provide: monthly P&Ls and balance sheets for 36 months, the trial balance and general ledger, AR/AP aging, the fixed-asset register and depreciation schedule, revenue recognition policy, customer concentration, inventory valuation method, and support for the proposed add-backs (related-party rent normalized to market). We also need payroll detail and any related-party rent to normalize Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc.'s earnings to a $2477406 EBITDA basis.

communications.subject: QoE findings and working-capital peg — Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc.
communications.summary: Accountant reports QoE findings and recommends the working-capital target.
communications.body: Our review supports adjusted EBITDA near $2477406 after normalizing related-party rent normalized to market, though we recommend reserving for a customer-concentration risk. Normalized working capital averages about $214992; we suggest pegging the target there with a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing. We note the seller's cash-basis reporting and have converted to accrual for comparability. These findings support the a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital underwriting.

communications.subject: Credit questions — Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. acquisition
communications.summary: Lender asks financing and accounting questions before commitment.
communications.body: Before we issue a commitment for the a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital of $7573808, we need: the QoE report, the debt-service-coverage projection on post-close cash flow, confirmation of the a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price and any no seller financing (which we will require to be on full standby), the Form 8594 allocation, and a personal financial statement. We also need to confirm collateral values for machinery, WIP inventory, and the trade name and that only specified trade payables and customer deposits are assumed.

communications.subject: Closing and funds flow — Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc.
communications.summary: Escrow agent confirms the closing statement, allocation, and holdback.
communications.body: Closing for the Casco Bay Landscaping, Inc. asset sale is set for 2026-05-16. The funds flow reflects the $6897794 price, the a bank term loan with a revolving line for working capital advance of $7573808, any no seller financing, the a 12-month holdback of 8% of the price retained here, and the closing working-capital true-up per a dollar-for-dollar working-capital true-up against a pegged target at closing. The bill of sale, assignment and assumption of specified trade payables and customer deposits, a new 10-year lease with the seller as landlord, a 3-year non-compete plus a 1-year consulting agreement, and the agreed Form 8594 allocation (an IRC § 1060 purchase-price allocation on Form 8594 across Class I-VII assets) will be delivered at closing into Downeast Dental Partners LLC.
